shortDescription: A bright three-bedroom semi-detached house with a south-facing garden, five minutes from Bromley South station.
longDescription: This well-proportioned semi-detached home sits on a quiet residential road within easy reach of Bromley town centre. The ground floor opens into a double reception room with original cornicing, leading through to an extended kitchen-diner with bi-fold doors onto the garden. Upstairs are three double bedrooms and a recently refitted family bathroom. The south-facing garden runs to approximately 60ft and includes a brick-built outbuilding, currently used as a home office. Bromley South station is a seven-minute walk, with fast services into London Victoria in under twenty minutes. The property is offered chain-free.

shortDescription: A second-floor two-bedroom apartment in a modern development, with a private balcony and allocated parking.
longDescription: Located on the second floor of a well-managed 2018 development, this apartment offers open-plan living with a full-width balcony overlooking landscaped communal gardens. The kitchen is fitted with integrated Bosch appliances and quartz worktops. Both bedrooms are doubles, with the principal bedroom benefiting from fitted wardrobes and an en-suite shower room. The flat comes with one allocated parking space, a video entry system, and lift access to all floors. The lease has 118 years remaining with a service charge of approximately £1,450 per annum.

shortDescription: A substantial five-bedroom Victorian townhouse arranged over four floors, with a walled garden.
longDescription: A rare opportunity to acquire a period townhouse on one of Camden's most sought-after crescents. Arranged over four floors, the house retains a wealth of original features including marble fireplaces, sash windows and a sweeping staircase. The lower ground floor houses a large kitchen-family room opening onto a walled garden. The first-floor drawing room spans the full width of the building with three floor-to-ceiling windows. Five bedrooms and three bathrooms occupy the upper floors, with the principal suite taking the entire second floor. Regent's Park and Primrose Hill are both within a ten-minute walk.

shortDescription: A compact one-bedroom flat with a west-facing living room, moments from King's Cross and the British Library.
longDescription: A neatly presented one-bedroom flat on the third floor of a purpose-built block. The living room faces west and holds afternoon light, with an adjoining galley kitchen. The double bedroom accommodates a king-size bed with room for storage, and the bathroom was refitted in 2023. Residents benefit from a secure bike store and access to a shared roof terrace. King's Cross St Pancras is an eight-minute walk, making this a strong option for a first-time buyer or a buy-to-let investor. No onward chain.

shortDescription: A four-bedroom 1930s family home backing onto parkland, with a converted loft and off-street parking.
longDescription: Set back from the road behind a block-paved driveway, this 1930s house has been extended to the rear and converted at loft level. The ground floor comprises a bay-fronted reception room, a separate dining room and a 22ft kitchen-breakfast room with a lantern roof. Three bedrooms and a family bathroom sit on the first floor, with a fourth bedroom and en-suite in the loft. The rear garden backs directly onto Sutcliffe Park, giving an unusual sense of openness for the area. Blackheath village and its station are a fifteen-minute walk.

shortDescription: A three-bedroom riverside apartment on the eleventh floor, with dock views and a 24-hour concierge.
longDescription: Positioned on the eleventh floor of a landmark riverside tower, this apartment offers panoramic views across Millwall Dock and towards the City. The open-plan reception spans nearly 30ft with floor-to-ceiling glazing and access to a private winter garden. Three bedrooms include a principal suite with a walk-in dressing area and en-suite. Building amenities include a 24-hour concierge, residents' gym, and a screening room. South Quay DLR is a four-minute walk, with Canary Wharf Elizabeth Line services close by. Lease: 989 years remaining.

shortDescription: A two-bedroom mid-terrace cottage with a courtyard garden, ideal for a first purchase.
longDescription: A charming mid-terrace cottage on a residential turning off Lower Addiscombe Road. The ground floor has been opened up to create a through lounge-diner with a wood-burning stove, leading to a modern kitchen at the rear. Two double bedrooms and a bathroom sit on the first floor. Outside, a low-maintenance courtyard garden is laid to porcelain paving with raised beds. East Croydon station is a twelve-minute walk, offering fast services to London Bridge and Victoria in under fifteen minutes. Offered with no onward chain.
